player_utterance: Grief's a strange host — it keeps the house full but makes everyone feel alone.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup of cold coffee in one hand, the other in his jacket pocket

player_utterance: We don't need introductions tonight, if that's alright with you. I'm just someone who turns up where people are carrying things.

believer_state_after.posture: standing, weight shifted onto one foot, cup lowered to his side

player_utterance: You sound like a man who's made peace with mystery — or learned to joke about it, which isn't quite the same thing.

believer_state_after.posture: standing, head tilted slightly, eyes steady on the stranger

player_utterance: That candle's been working harder than most people here tonight — holding still while everything moves around it.

believer_state_after.posture: standing by the small table, eyes on the candle, one hand resting lightly on the back of the bench
sitting on the bench, fingers laced, looking at the candle, not at the stranger
sitting on the bench, elbows now on his knees, hands loose between them

voice_offers.read: He's not fleeing; the silence is doing real work.
He looked at the candle instead of answering. That's the seam.
voice_offers.line: You teach the Reformation for a living. So why won't you say his name tonight?

player_utterance: We know where he is now — and you don't.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, very still, looking straight ahead at nothing in particular

voice_offers.read: He bent forward; the question already landed without answer.
His silence is the door — name what he won't.
voice_offers.line: It's your son, isn't it. You haven't said his name once tonight.

player_utterance: You let him have the room. What would it have cost to take up a little of it yourself?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, leaning forward, elbows on knees, hands clasped tight

voice_offers.read: He won't answer. Name the silence as the answer.
voice_offers.line: That silence is louder than anything you taught this year.

voice_offers.read: He knows where his son is and won't say.
voice_offers.line: You know the address. You just won't go to it.

player_utterance: You've named the costs to you. You haven't named him.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, head lowered, one hand at his mouth, the other gripping his knee

voice_offers.read: He's curled around it; one true question opens him.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. Just once tonight. Say it.

voice_offers.read: Pin the teacher to his own doctrine about presence.
voice_offers.line: You teach that the word made flesh matters. So whose flesh are you refusing to name?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting very still on the bench, head coming up slowly, eyes finding the stranger in the dusk

voice_offers.read: Three silences. He's behind the hand. One push lands now.
voice_offers.line: You can grip that knee all night. The name still won't come, will it.

voice_offers.read: He's already inside it. Pressing now is hitting a kneeling man.
voice_offers.line: Whatever his name is — you don't have to say it to me. Just to yourself.

voice_offers.read: He's quoting to hide. Name the evasion itself.
voice_offers.line: You've been quoting other men for an hour. None of them had a son.

player_utterance: You're looking at me now. That's something. I'm not in a hurry.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hands open on his knees, gaze steady on the stranger but not pressing

voice_offers.read: The name is right there; one more push lands it.
voice_offers.line: Ray had a son. You know his name. I'm waiting.

voice_offers.read: He is looking up at last; meet the look, not the wound.
voice_offers.line: You're looking at me now. That's something. I'm not in a hurry.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, gaze drifted down to the seams of the courtyard stones

voice_offers.read: He's met my eyes; let the quiet keep working.
He's steady now — one clean ask lands.
voice_offers.line: The boy's name. When you're ready.

player_utterance: Whatever you're seeing in those stones — I'll wait for it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, hand still resting on the underside of the seat, thumb tracing a groove he can't see

voice_offers.read: Two silences taken. He's softening. One quiet question lands now.
He's looking at stones, not fleeing. Meet him there, not at the wound.
voice_offers.line: Whatever you're seeing in those stones — I'll wait for it.

player_utterance: Whose name is under your thumb right now?

believer_state_after.posture: half-turned on the bench, looking down at his own hand where it rests on the wood

voice_offers.read: Three silences taken; patience is becoming its own evasion.
voice_offers.line: John. The groove under the bench — whose name are you tracing?

voice_offers.read: He retreats into quotation; pin him to his own words.
voice_offers.line: You taught your students Luther on confession. What was the line about the unspoken sin?

player_utterance: You've been keeping your hand on him this whole time.

believer_state_after.posture: both hands flat on knees, palms down, head bowed, shoulders drawn in

voice_offers.read: He is breaking; pressing now risks shattering, not opening.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to say it out loud. I'm here.

voice_offers.read: His thumb is on the name; one more breath finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Say it. Just the name. That's all.

believer_state_after.posture: hands open on knees, head bowed, very still

voice_offers.read: He's bowed and breaking; pressure now would be cruelty.
He's at the edge; one true word lands him.
voice_offers.line: You've been carrying him alone. Let him out.
